description: Triggering and output-quality probes for graded exercise generation with separated solutions.
should_trigger: Give me exercises on backpropagation, easy to hard, with solutions so I can check myself.
Quiz me on the EM algorithm — I want to know if I actually understand it.
I just finished reading the LoRA paper. Test whether I really got it.
Make me practice problems on measure-theoretic probability, from basic to advanced, and keep the answers separate.
How do I check if I understood diffusion models? Some problems would help.
I'm learning PPO. Build a self-check that goes from definitions up to deriving the objective.
should_not_trigger: Teach me how attention works from scratch.
What baselines and benchmarks do I need for my new method?
Help me design an ablation study for my paper.
Walk me through a learning path for reinforcement learning — I'm brand new.
Write the intro section of my paper.
output_checks: Produces two separate artifacts: exercises.md (problems only) and solutions.md (answers + rubric).
Problems are tagged by difficulty tier (1-4) and concept.
The set spans tiers and includes at least one Tier 3+ problem.
Each solution includes answer, why, a pass rubric, a common-wrong-turn note, and a revisit pointer.
States the gate rule that understanding is verified only when Tier 3+ passes unaided.
Exercises are grounded in the learner's actual source material, not invented facts.
